name: OpenHands (All Hands AI)
pricing_notes: Open source self-hosted; All Hands Cloud: usage-based (LLM costs pass-through + platform fee)

auth.notes: Self-hosted; configure LLM provider credentials in config.toml

performance.notes: Task duration minutes to hours; Docker-based sandbox for code execution

agent_readiness.notes: Open-source Devin alternative. Sandboxed Docker execution environment. Supports all LLM providers. Web UI + CLI + Python API. Headless mode for CI/CD. GitHub integration. SWE-bench competitive performance.

gotchas: Requires Docker for sandboxed execution — adds setup complexity
Success rate highly dependent on LLM model choice
Long-running tasks can consume significant LLM tokens
No managed hosting without All Hands Cloud
best_when: Teams wanting Devin-like capabilities without $500+/mo subscription
Self-hosted deployments requiring full data control
Open-source projects where commercial SWE agents are impractical
Evaluating autonomous coding agents before committing to paid options
avoid_when: Teams wanting managed, no-ops solution (use Devin or SWE-agent services)
summary: OpenHands (formerly OpenDevin) is the leading open-source autonomous software engineering agent. SWE-bench competitive performance with commercial alternatives like Devin. Runs in Docker sandbox, supports all major LLM providers, includes web UI and Python API. Best open-source option for teams wanting autonomous coding capability without vendor lock-in or high subscription costs.
